title: Quasar Labs
compiled_truth:
Quasar Labs is a cybersecurity startup founded in 2024 by [Sol Mensah](people/sol-mensah), focused on post-quantum cryptography migration tooling and enterprise key management. The company's core thesis is straightforward: the quantum threat timeline is shorter than most orgs assume, and the migration path from classical to post-quantum cryptographic primitives is brutally complex at scale. Quasar builds tooling that audits existing cryptographic dependencies across codebases and infrastructure, generates migration plans, and manages the lifecycle of hybrid key schemes during transition periods.

The founding team came out of government-adjacent security work — Sol had spent years thinking about cryptographic agility before deciding the problem needed a dedicated product company. Early backing came from [Bex Andersen](people/bex-andersen), who led the pre-seed, along with [Nash Moreau](people/nash-moreau) and [Zara Lindqvist](people/zara-lindqvist) participating as angel investors. Bex reportedly pushed Sol to narrow the initial product surface to just key management before expanding into broader migration automation, which proved to be the right sequencing.

By mid-2027, Quasar Labs had landed contracts with several regulated financial institutions and a handful of defense-adjacent firms. Their key management layer — sometimes referred to internally as "QKM" — supports lattice-based and hash-based signature schemes alongside classical algorithms in a hybrid mode that lets enterprises transition gradually without hard cutover dates. The compliance story is strong: Quasar's audit trail and reporting tools make it relatively painless for security teams to demonstrate cryptographic posture to regulators.

The intersection with Anchor Codec's world is interesting. Quasar has been exploring how deterministic data infrastructure could simplify certain aspects of their key distribution and verification pipelines. There were preliminary conversations in late 2027 about whether Anchor's single-pass primitives could reduce overhead in Quasar's hybrid key negotiation flows. Nothing formalized yet, but the technical teams have been talking.

Quasar is still relatively small — around 30 people as of early 2028 — but growing steadily. Sol runs a tight ship and has been deliberate about not over-hiring ahead of revenue. The company raised a Series A in mid-2027 that gave them roughly 18 months of runway. They're not flashy, but in post-quantum circles they're increasingly well-regarded. The product works, the migration tooling saves months of manual effort, and the team actualy ships.
timeline:
- **2026-03-15** | Quasar Labs closes pre-seed round led by [Bex Andersen](people/bex-andersen) with participation from [Nash Moreau](people/nash-moreau) and [Zara Lindqvist](people/zara-lindqvist).
- **2026-06-22** | First version of QKM (Quasar Key Manager) enters private beta with two financial services firms.
- **2026-10-08** | [Sol Mensah](people/sol-mensah) presents at CryptoAgility Summit on hybrid key management challenges in regulated environments.
- **2027-01-19** | Quasar ships cryptographic audit tooling as a standalone product, expanding beyond pure key management.
- **2027-04-11** | Headcount reaches 20; company opens second office in a European city to be closer to regulatory customers.
- **2027-07-03** | Series A closes. Terms not publicly disclosed but rumored at $14M.
- **2027-09-28** | First defense-adjacent contract signed after passing extensive security review process.
- **2027-11-14** | Preliminary technical discussions with Anchor Codec team about integrating deterministic verification into QKM's key distribution layer.
- **2028-02-06** | Quasar Labs featured in a major infrastructure security report as a leading post-quantum migration vendor.
- **2028-03-20** | [Sol Mensah](people/sol-mensah) and Anchor Codec engineers meet at a working session to scope potential integration work around single-pass authentication primitives.

_facts.name: Quasar Labs
_facts.description: Post-quantum cryptography migration tooling and key management
